In this lesson, I want to show you how to set up the automation portion of the autoresponder chain. Now, before I show you exactly how to do this, I want to show you a document and you're going to get a copy of this document, so don't worry, you'll be able to download it below this video. But this is kind of the behind the scenes inner workings, if you will, of the automation behind the amplification autoresponder chain. So basically, let me kind of just walk you through this very quickly and then I'll show you how simple this is to set up. So when somebody enters their email address into your traffic captivation page, they're going to get subscribed to list zero. Now that'll happen through one of two ways. If you're using something like lead pages, well lead pages integrates with a Weber and so when you're creating your traffic captivation page inside of lead pages, you'll be able to just off of a dropdown, you'll be able to say, subscribe this person to list zero. You won't have to do any kind of anything with code or any kind of opt-in pages or you won't have to use any of the sign up forms in a Weber. But now, when somebody purchases the customer captivation offer, there's a few things that we want to have happen, three things specifically. The first is that we want to unsubscribe them from the prospect list. Then we want to subscribe them to list one and subscribe them to list C. So they buy the product and so we're going to subscribe them to list number one that you now know and you now know what that list is. We're going to subscribe them to list number one and we're going to unsubscribe them from the prospect sequence and we're going to subscribe them to the customer sequence. So again, in essence, we're unsubscribing them from one and subscribing them to two. Now if somebody takes the reverse add-on offer, they're unsubscribed from list one. You can see how it becomes a chain because every offer that they take, it unsubscribes them from the previous one. So they take the reverse add-on offer, they get unsubscribed from the customer captivation chain and they get subscribed to list two. Now if they take the soft offer add-on, they get unsubscribed from this, unsubscribed from this because let's say that and the reason why I want you to understand why something like, you know, it says unsubscribed from list one even though it also says unsubscribed from list one here is that if they don't purchase the reverse add-on offer but go on to the soft offer add-on and they do purchase the soft offer add-on, that's when we have to remove them from any of the other sequences. We've got to make sure that we remove them from list one and list two and then put them on to list three. Now if they go on and they purchase the mid-tier add-on, we're going to unsubscribe them from the first three lists and we're going to subscribe them to list number four. So you can see how it is all automated based on what it is that they choose and if they take the mid-tier or when they purchase, excuse me, the thank you gift offer, they get unsubscribed from all of these from the mid-tier list, the soft offer list, the reverse add-on list and the customer captivation list and they just get subscribed to list five. And so this down here and this will all be labeled when you get this, this down here is for the premium offer. If they take the premium offer, they get unsubscribed from the main customer list. Now remember that main customer list is they don't get to the webinar for 10 days and then they get the premium offer. And so they would get unsubscribed from the premium offer list and then they would get subscribed to the premium offer thank you list, list number six. And so let me show you how easy it is to set up this kind of automation inside of a Weber. Now keep in mind that what I want you to understand is that when somebody buys the reverse upsell, you see how it says subscribe to list when they purchase this, all of these subscriptions where it says subscribed, subscribed and subscribed, these all happen for you automatically with SamCart. So if somebody takes advantage of the reverse add-on, SamCart, you know, SamCart as you've seen will subscribe them to list two. Then with what I'm going to show you inside of a Weber, what we just want to do is say when this person gets subscribed to list two, unsubscribe them from list one. So really in essence, just so that you understand this, in essence SamCart is taking care of all of these, the subscribes and the automation rules that I'm going to show you now are taking care of the unsubscribes. So the unsubscribes are what you set up the automation to do inside of a Weber and the subscribes will all happen with SamCart. So let me show you how easy this is to do. So when you're setting up your chain, when you're setting up the actual actions for each of your different sequences, what you want to do is one at a time, you want to go into each list. So remember, we're working right now in this list one. Now under list one, we want to go over to list options and list automation. Now what this is, is this is automation rules that we're going to set up for this particular list. For example, when we are going to work with the list one, for example, when we're going to subscribe somebody to list one, like they opt in, for example, to your traffic activation page, you just want to make sure that you are operating under list one in a Weber. Now here's the way these rules work. It's very simple and very straightforward. There are three different automation rules that you have. You can unsubscribe somebody from this page, excuse me, from this list, from this sequence, when they subscribe to, and then you can choose the list. So on this drop down list, you're going to have your eight different lists. You're going to have list zero, list C, and then list one, two, three, four, five, and six. And so you can set a rule that says, I want you to unsubscribe somebody from this particular list when they are subscribed to this list. So for example, when we are saying that we want to unsubscribe somebody from list zero, when they are subscribed to list one, all you're doing is going in here and saying, we're under list one. I want to unsubscribe from list one. Actually let's do this. This is list zero, and now we can say, let's get rid of this just for one second. Now we could say we're under list zero. We're in the customer, we're in the prospect follow up list. So we can say create a rule that says unsubscribe from this list, list zero, when somebody is subscribing to list one. List one meaning they took the customer captivation offer. And so all we've got to do is just add that rule. Now we can do more with this. So you see how, all we did here was say unsubscribe them from this when they are purchased. So we're purchasing the customer captivation offer. So what we've just done, so let me kind of paint this global picture for you. So somebody, when they buy the customer, let's back up actually even more, when they opt into your traffic captivation page, they will automatically be subscribed to list zero. Now they go to your customer captivation offer and they buy it. And so what happens is now Sam Cart will take the money and will subscribe them to list one. Then we need to go into a Weber and we need to set up an automation rule, which we just did that says unsubscribe from list zero when we purchase, when somebody purchases the customer captivation offer. Now just keep in mind that inside of a Weber, when purchased is really saying when they are subscribed to, that's how a Weber does it. So you can see again under the automation rule, when somebody is subscribed or unsubscribed to a list, you can unsubscribe or subscribe them to a different list. Then what you would do is you would, so right? So in this case, Sam Cart is going to subscribe them to list one when they buy the customer captivation offer. We just set up the automation rule to unsubscribe somebody from list, um, list zero. Now what we need to do is say subscribe to list C when somebody is subscribed to list one. And so what you would do is you would go to list one and then what you would say, subscribe to, um, excuse me, I'm sorry, you'd go to list C, the customer list, and you would say subscribe to list C when subscribing to list one. If that makes sense, we're under list one right now. So, so in other words, and then you're adding that automation rule. And so all you're doing is just using these automation rules inside of a Weber to make sure that people are unsubscribed and subscribed to the right lists. Now let me clarify a couple of quick things here for you. There's only one time where an individual gets subscribed to another list via the automation rules and that's right here. And so what happens is Sam Cart will put them on list one. We will, we will use an automation rule to unsubscribe them from list zero and we will use another automation rule to also subscribe them to list C. Other than that, there are no other times where we use the, excuse me, the automation rules to subscribe to a list. The rest of the time it's always unsubscribing. And so in this case, what happens is Sam Cart will subscribe them to this list if they buy. Sam Cart will subscribe them to this list. Sam Cart will subscribe them to this one, to this one, to this one. You'll just be setting up the, the automation rules for the unsubscribes. You'll be saying that when somebody subscribes to list three, set up an automation rule to unsubscribe them from list one, set up another, another automation rule to unsubscribe them from list two. When Sam Cart subscribes somebody to list four because they've bought the mid tier, you'll go in and you'll set up the automation rule so that it unsubscribes from list one, another automation rule to unsubscribe them from list two, and another automation rule to unsubscribe them from list three, and so on. And that's how you automate the moving of individuals from one list to another list to another list for the second chance offers as part of the transaction maximization sequence.
